channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Federal cyber leaders on building national resilience
item.description: How are you building resilience into your agency’s cybersecurity? The post Federal cyber leaders on building national resilience first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: New research shows a quarter of freshwater animals are threatened with extinction
item.description: New research shows about a quarter of animals living in rivers, lakes and other freshwater sources are threatened with extinction.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New US CIO appointments, January 2025
item.description: Movers & Shakers is where you can keep up with new CIO appointments and gain valuable insight into the job market and CIO hiring trends.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Uber is replacing Lyft in a points partnership with Delta
item.description: Lyft and Delta are ending their points-earning loyalty partnership in April and the airline will start a new program with rideshare competitor Uber. Uber announced the multiyear strategic partnership on Tuesday evening at the CES tech conference in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Transformación digital en 2025: qué está de moda y qué no
item.description: Algunos sostienen que la aparición de la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa ha hecho que la transformación digital sea cosa del pasado. Transformación de IA es el término que se utiliza para describirla.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CIO y CCO: la importancia de la comunicación en proyectos TI
item.description: Una de las claves para garantizar una buena implementación de cualquier iniciativa tecnológica no tiene nada que ver con la propia tecnología: es la comunicación.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Good news, gamers: Playing ‘The Legend of Zelda’ helps mental health
item.description: Next time someone claims staying in and playing video games is bad for you, direct them to this study that suggests open-world games might actually improve your mental health.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Fact-checking on Facebook was already a lost cause
item.description: On January 7, Mark Zuckebrerg made a not-so-stunning announcement. The Meta CEO said that his company is ending its third-party fact-checking program, declaring that “experts, like everyone else, have their own biases and perspectives.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Silicon Valley Nonprofit Sues Microsoft Over Email Account Dispute
item.description: LifeMoves, a Silicon Valley nonprofit that helps homeless people, has sued Microsoft over a dispute involving email accounts and cloud applications.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Varian, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta's fact-checking changes put company under political microscope
item.description: Meta’s decision to eliminate its fact-checking program marks a sharp reversal for the major social media company, quickly prompting questions over the firm’s direction as President-elect Trump heads back to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Data Center Power Efficiency: A New Angle on Networking Cables
item.description: Data center operators seek to boost power efficiency and sustainability, with a focus on cutting electricity bills through innovative technologies like Point2 Technology's smart retimer SoCs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Federal IT Skills Shortage: How AI Can Help Relieve the Gap and Improve Security
item.description: The global IT skills shortage is predicted to impact 90% of organizations by 2026, according to IDC. The number of cybersecurity vacancies is particularly concerning in federal IT and security departments.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: AI detects ovarian cancer better than human doctors: Study
item.description: AI based models achieved an accuracy rate of 86% for detecting ovarian cancer, compared to 82% for human experts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Almost nothing remains of Software AG
item.description: Software AG (now legally known as Software GmbH) has sold its Alfabet and Cumulocity divisions, following on from the recent sales of webMethods, StreamSets and TrendMiner.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Biggest Nvidia takeaways from Jensen Huang's CES 2025 keynote
item.description: Nvidia’s stock is on the upswing after its CEO, Jensen Huang, unveiled a suite of new products, services and partnerships at CES 2025.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Facebook to end fact-checking, boost political content
item.description: The company said automated moderation would focus on serious issues like terrorism and child sexual exploitation instead.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Trump ally Dana White among Meta’s 3 new directors
item.description: Tapping White to join the board could be seen as Mark Zuckerberg’s latest maneuver to improve ties with Trump.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Why Apple’s AI-driven reality distortion matters
item.description: Apple has been forced to admit what every company involved in artificial intelligence (AI) should also be forced to state — AI makes mistakes, just like people do. On the surface, it’s not a terribly big deal: Apple’s AI badly mangled a handful of news headlines.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities
item.description: The White House is prepping a new cybersecurity executive order that would address cloud security, give CISA new authorities to do threat hunting and more. The post White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Keep yourself and your money safe from these four scams
item.description: Authorities are warning people to be wary of pig butchering scams, travel scams, law enforcement scams and utility scams to prevent losing money to fraudsters.
item.dc:creator: Nollyanne Delacruz, The Mercury News

item.title: FBI's new warning about AI-driven scams that are after your cash
item.description: FBI warns that criminals are using generative AI to exploit individuals with deceptive tactics. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains their tactics and how to protect yourself from them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia's new GPU series led an avalanche of entertainment-related announcements at CES
item.description: Nvidia unveiled its GeForce RTX 50 Series desktop and laptop GPUs — powered by its new Blackwell artificial intelligence chip — kicking off a string of entertainment-related AI announcements and discussions at the trade show, including several terse conversations about regulation of the budding tech
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Agentic AI: The top challenges and how to overcome them
item.description: As generative AI continues to surge in popularity, we are already seeing it evolve into the next generation of machine-learning-driven technology: agentic AI. With agentic AI, we are not just prompting models and receiving an answer in a simple one-step process.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft will invest $80B in AI data centers in fiscal 2025
item.description: Microsoft is on track to invest around $80 billion this fiscal year to build out AI-enabled data centers, company president Brad Smith said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government
item.description: Guy Cavallo has been the Office of Personnel Management’s CIO since 2020 and also worked at SBA, TSA and began his career working for the Senate. The post OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: It is easier than ever to disable Location Sharing on your Android phone
item.description: Google Maps Location Sharing is now built into Android settings; no need for Maps settings. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses the welcome improvement for Android users.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to expect from NaaS in 2025
item.description: Enterprises have been slow to adopt NaaS for a number of reasons, including lack of clarity on exactly what NaaS is, lack of a solid business case that NaaS saves money over the long term, and questions around security, visibility, control, and data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Linux filesystems: Ext4, Btrfs, XFS, ZFS and more
item.description: Linux supports quite a few filesystem types. Your choice of which filesystem to use can play an important role in optimizing performance, reliability and data integrity. Among the more popular filesystems are Ext4, Btrfs, XFS and ZFS. Each is widely used and offers some unique features and benefits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Appeals court blocks return of US net neutrality rules for ISPs
item.description: The reintroduction of neutrality rules for network providers is looking increasingly unlikely, at least for the next five years, after a US appeals court blocked efforts by the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to reinstate them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Turo rentals emerge as common thread in Las Vegas Cybertruck and New Orleans deadly incidents
item.description: Turo faces scrutiny after tragic incidents involving rented vehicles, raising questions about security measures and regulatory frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China rolls out its crime-fighting ball to chase down criminals
item.description: A Chinese company has developed a spherical, 276-pound law enforcement robot that has been chasing down criminals at impressive speeds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Confronting the growing drone threat
item.description: There is a solid rationale for prioritizing safe, secure and controlled RF cyber-based counter-UAS technology as decision makers consider future standards. The post Confronting the growing drone threat first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
